slices.name: TestConfigLoading
slices.code:
20 | class TestConfigLoading(unittest.TestCase):
  21 |     def test_default_config_skeleton_includes_expected_keys(self):
  22 |         skeleton = default_config_skeleton()
  23 |         self.assertIn("schema_version", skeleton)
  24 |         self.assertIn("project_identity", skeleton)
  25 |         self.assertIn("profiles", skeleton)
  26 | 
  27 |     def test_load_json_config_reads_valid_json(self):
  28 |         with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", delete=False, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
  29 |             json.dump({"schema_version": "1.0"}, handle)
  30 |             path = pathlib.Path(handle.name)
  31 |         try:
  32 |             config = load_json_config(path)
  33 |             self.assertEqual(config["schema_version"], "1.0")
  34 |         finally:
  35 |             path.unlink()
  36 | 
  37 |     def test_load_json_config_raises_on_invalid_json(self):
  38 |         with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", delete=False, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
  39 |             handle.write("{ invalid json }")
  40 |             path = pathlib.Path(handle.name)
  41 |         try:
  42 |             with self.assertRaises(ConfigError):
  43 |                 load_json_config(path)
  44 |         finally:
  45 |             path.unlink()
  46 | 
  47 |     def test_load_json_config_raises_on_missing_file(self):
  48 |         with self.assertRaises(FileNotFoundError):
  49 |             load_json_config(pathlib.Path("does_not_exist.json"))

slices.name: TestValidateConfig
slices.code:
52 | class TestValidateConfig(unittest.TestCase):
  53 |     """validate_config() accepts well-formed configs and rejects structural violations."""
  54 | 
  55 |     def test_valid_minimal_config_passes(self):
  56 |         """A config with only schema_version should validate without error."""
  57 |         validate_config({"schema_version": "1.0"})
  58 | 
  59 |     def test_valid_full_skeleton_passes(self):
  60 |         """The default skeleton must always pass validation."""
  61 |         validate_config(default_config_skeleton())
  62 | 
  63 |     def test_missing_schema_version_raises(self):
  64 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
  65 |             validate_config({"profiles": {}})
  66 |         self.assertIn("schema_version", str(ctx.exception))
  67 | 
  68 |     def test_non_string_schema_version_raises(self):
  69 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
  70 |             validate_config({"schema_version": 1})
  71 |         self.assertIn("schema_version", str(ctx.exception))
  72 | 
  73 |     def test_profiles_as_list_raises(self):
  74 |         """profiles must be a dict, not a list."""
  75 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
  76 |             validate_config({"schema_version": "1.0", "profiles": ["default"]})
  77 |         self.assertIn("profiles", str(ctx.exception))
  78 | 
  79 |     def test_non_dict_profile_entry_raises(self):
  80 |         """Each value inside profiles must be a dict."""
  81 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
  82 |             validate_config({
  83 |                 "schema_version": "1.0",
  84 |                 "profiles": {"bad_profile": "just-a-string"},
  85 |             })
  86 |         self.assertIn("bad_profile", str(ctx.exception))
  87 | 
  88 |     def test_source_scope_as_dict_raises(self):
  89 |         """source_scope must be a list, not a dict."""
  90 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
  91 |             validate_config({"schema_version": "1.0", "source_scope": {"key": "val"}})
  92 |         self.assertIn("source_scope", str(ctx.exception))
  93 | 
  94 |     def test_optional_sections_absent_passes(self):
  95 |         """A config with schema_version only must pass — all other sections are optional."""
  96 |         validate_config({"schema_version": "1.0"})

slices.name: TestResolveProfile
slices.code:
99 | class TestResolveProfile(unittest.TestCase):
 100 |     """resolve_profile() returns the right profile dict and raises on unknown names."""
 101 | 
 102 |     _CFG = {
 103 |         "schema_version": "1.0",
 104 |         "profiles": {
 105 |             "default": {"include_exts": [".py"], "max_rows_soft": 500},
 106 |             "ci":      {"include_exts": [".py", ".toml"], "max_rows_hard": 1000},
 107 |         },
 108 |     }
 109 | 
 110 |     def test_known_profile_returned(self):
 111 |         profile = resolve_profile(self._CFG, "default")
 112 |         self.assertEqual(profile["include_exts"], [".py"])
 113 |         self.assertEqual(profile["max_rows_soft"], 500)
 114 | 
 115 |     def test_second_profile_returned(self):
 116 |         profile = resolve_profile(self._CFG, "ci")
 117 |         self.assertIn(".toml", profile["include_exts"])
 118 | 
 119 |     def test_unknown_profile_raises(self):
 120 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
 121 |             resolve_profile(self._CFG, "nonexistent")
 122 |         self.assertIn("nonexistent", str(ctx.exception))
 123 | 
 124 |     def test_error_message_lists_available_profiles(self):
 125 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError) as ctx:
 126 |             resolve_profile(self._CFG, "missing")
 127 |         msg = str(ctx.exception)
 128 |         self.assertIn("default", msg)
 129 |         self.assertIn("ci", msg)
 130 | 
 131 |     def test_empty_profiles_section_raises(self):
 132 |         with self.assertRaises(ConfigError):
 133 |             resolve_profile({"schema_version": "1.0", "profiles": {}}, "default")

 113 |         self.assertEqual(profile["max_rows_soft"], 500)

slices.name: TestResolvePrecedence
slices.code:
136 | class TestResolvePrecedence(unittest.TestCase):
 137 |     """resolve_precedence() merges four layers with CLI winning over all others."""
 138 | 
 139 |     def test_cli_overrides_all_lower_layers(self):
 140 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 141 |             cli_overrides={"max_file_size": 100},
 142 |             profile_settings={"max_file_size": 200},
 143 |             project_defaults={"max_file_size": 300},
 144 |             builtin_defaults={"max_file_size": 400},
 145 |         )
 146 |         self.assertEqual(result["max_file_size"], 100)
 147 | 
 148 |     def test_profile_overrides_project_and_builtin(self):
 149 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 150 |             cli_overrides=None,
 151 |             profile_settings={"max_file_size": 200},
 152 |             project_defaults={"max_file_size": 300},
 153 |             builtin_defaults={"max_file_size": 400},
 154 |         )
 155 |         self.assertEqual(result["max_file_size"], 200)
 156 | 
 157 |     def test_project_overrides_builtin(self):
 158 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 159 |             cli_overrides=None,
 160 |             profile_settings=None,
 161 |             project_defaults={"max_file_size": 300},
 162 |             builtin_defaults={"max_file_size": 400},
 163 |         )
 164 |         self.assertEqual(result["max_file_size"], 300)
 165 | 
 166 |     def test_builtin_used_when_all_higher_absent(self):
 167 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 168 |             cli_overrides=None,
 169 |             profile_settings=None,
 170 |             project_defaults=None,
 171 |             builtin_defaults={"max_file_size": 400},
 172 |         )
 173 |         self.assertEqual(result["max_file_size"], 400)
 174 | 
 175 |     def test_none_value_falls_through_to_lower_layer(self):
 176 |         """An explicit None in a higher layer must fall through, not win."""
 177 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 178 |             cli_overrides={"max_file_size": None},
 179 |             profile_settings={"max_file_size": None},
 180 |             project_defaults={"max_file_size": 300},
 181 |             builtin_defaults={"max_file_size": 400},
 182 |         )
 183 |         self.assertEqual(result["max_file_size"], 300)
 184 | 
 185 |     def test_false_zero_do_not_fall_through(self):
 186 |         """False and 0 are valid values and must NOT fall through to lower layers."""
 187 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 188 |             cli_overrides={"flag": False, "count": 0},
 189 |             profile_settings={"flag": True, "count": 99},
 190 |             project_defaults={},
 191 |             builtin_defaults={},
 192 |         )
 193 |         self.assertIs(result["flag"], False)
 194 |         self.assertEqual(result["count"], 0)
 195 | 
 196 |     def test_key_only_in_builtin_is_included(self):
 197 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 198 |             cli_overrides={},
 199 |             profile_settings={},
 200 |             project_defaults={},
 201 |             builtin_defaults={"only_here": "value"},
 202 |         )
 203 |         self.assertEqual(result["only_here"], "value")
 204 | 
 205 |     def test_all_none_layers_returns_empty(self):
 206 |         result = resolve_precedence(None, None, None, None)
 207 |         self.assertEqual(result, {})
 208 | 
 209 |     def test_keys_from_all_layers_are_merged(self):
 210 |         result = resolve_precedence(
 211 |             cli_overrides={"a": 1},
 212 |             profile_settings={"b": 2},
 213 |             project_defaults={"c": 3},
 214 |             builtin_defaults={"d": 4},
 215 |         )
 216 |         self.assertEqual(result, {"a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3, "d": 4})

slices.name: TestSchemaSyncProfileFormat
slices.code:
219 | class TestSchemaSyncProfileFormat(unittest.TestCase):
 220 |     """Schema-sync repair (Phase 7): profile 'format' field must validate cleanly."""
 221 | 
 222 |     def test_profile_with_format_json_validates(self):
 223 |         """A profile containing format:'json' must not raise ConfigError."""
 224 |         data = {
 225 |             "schema_version": "1.0",
 226 |             "profiles": {
 227 |                 "strict": {
 228 |                     "include_exts": [".py"],
 229 |                     "format": "json",
 230 |                 }
 231 |             },
 232 |         }
 233 |         # validate_config only checks structural types, not profile-internal keys,
 234 |         # so this is a smoke test that the dict itself is accepted.
 235 |         validate_config(data)
 236 | 
 237 |     def test_profile_with_format_text_validates(self):
 238 |         data = {
 239 |             "schema_version": "1.0",
 240 |             "profiles": {"default": {"format": "text"}},
 241 |         }
 242 |         validate_config(data)
 243 | 
 244 |     def test_profile_format_is_resolvable(self):
 245 |         """resolve_profile must return the format key intact."""
 246 |         data = {
 247 |             "schema_version": "1.0",
 248 |             "profiles": {"ci": {"format": "json", "deterministic": True}},
 249 |         }
 250 |         profile = resolve_profile(data, "ci")
 251 |         self.assertEqual(profile["format"], "json")
 252 |         self.assertTrue(profile["deterministic"])
 253 | 
 254 |     def test_validate_only_with_profile_format_key(self):
 255 |         """--validate-only via slicer v7 must succeed for a config with format in the profile."""
 256 |         import subprocess, sys, tempfile, pathlib
 257 |         _SLICER = str(pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "semantic_slicer_v7.0.py")
 258 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 259 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 260 |             cfg = tmp / "test_cfg.json"
 261 |             import json as _json
 262 |             cfg.write_text(_json.dumps({
 263 |                 "schema_version": "1.0",
 264 |                 "profiles": {"default": {"format": "json", "deterministic": True}},
 265 |             }), encoding="utf-8")
 266 |             result = subprocess.run(
 267 |                 [sys.executable, _SLICER, "--validate-only", "--config", str(cfg), "--task-profile", "default"],
 268 |                 cwd=str(pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]),
 269 |                 capture_output=True, text=True,
 270 |             )
 271 |         self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stdout + result.stderr)
 272 |         self.assertIn("validate-only check passed", result.stdout)

slices.name: test_profile_with_format_json_validates
test_profile_with_format_text_validates
test_profile_format_is_resolvable
slices.code:

 249 |         }

slices.name: TestExampleConfigs
slices.code:
275 | class TestExampleConfigs(unittest.TestCase):
 276 |     """Example config files must load and pass validate_config."""
 277 | 
 278 |     def _load_and_validate(self, filename: str) -> dict:
 279 |         path = _EXAMPLES_DIR / filename
 280 |         self.assertTrue(path.exists(), f"Example config not found: {path}")
 281 |         data = load_json_config(path)
 282 |         validate_config(data)   # must not raise
 283 |         return data
 284 | 
 285 |     def test_python_project_loads_and_validates(self):
 286 |         data = self._load_and_validate("python_project.json")
 287 |         self.assertEqual(data["schema_version"], "1.0")
 288 |         self.assertIn("profiles", data)
 289 | 
 290 |     def test_polyglot_runtime_loads_and_validates(self):
 291 |         data = self._load_and_validate("polyglot_runtime.json")
 292 |         self.assertEqual(data["schema_version"], "1.0")
 293 |         self.assertIn("profiles", data)
 294 | 
 295 |     def test_training_pipeline_loads_and_validates(self):
 296 |         data = self._load_and_validate("training_pipeline.json")
 297 |         self.assertEqual(data["schema_version"], "1.0")
 298 |         self.assertIn("profiles", data)
 299 |
